summary: Recent changes in the WebRTC area focus on improving memory management and resource handling for audio devices and media sources. By implementing the **MakeRefPtr** method, which helps with automatic memory management, Firefox enhances the stability and performance of its audio functionalities, particularly in handling various audio devices. Additionally, adjustments to finalize classes in the media section ensure that resources are correctly cleaned up, reducing the risk of memory leaks and thereby supporting smoother video calls and streaming experiences. Overall, these updates contribute to a more reliable and efficient browsing experience for users relying on real-time communication technologies.
